executiveSummary: This SaaS Subscription Agreement with CloudBase Inc. presents High overall risk (score: 72/100). The two most serious issues are a minimal liability cap (1× monthly fees = $200) that leaves us with nearly no recourse for service failure, and unilateral modification rights that allow CloudBase to change pricing or features with 30 days' notice mid-term. The 90-day auto-renewal notice window and fully non-refundable fees compound the financial exposure. Legal counsel review is recommended before execution. We should not sign as-is without negotiating the liability cap and requesting an SLA addendum.

biggestRisks.explanation: The 1× monthly fee cap means our maximum recovery from CloudBase for any failure — including data loss — is $200. This is the most important clause to renegotiate.
CloudBase can change the product, pricing, or terms with 30 days' notice. If we reject the change, we forfeit remaining prepaid fees.
The service is provided 'as is' with no uptime commitment. Extended outages cost us in productivity with no contractual recourse.
We must cancel by October 15, 2024 to avoid auto-renewal. Missing this deadline locks us into another $2,400 payment.

financialObligations: 1.5% monthly late fee if payment is delayed
Full annual fee owed again if auto-renewal notice window is missed
Potential uncapped fee increase on renewal (60-day notice)
questionsForLawyer: Can we negotiate the liability cap to at least 12× monthly fees, with data breach and gross negligence carve-outs?
Will CloudBase accept a mutual written amendment requirement for any fee or feature changes?
Is the class action waiver in the arbitration clause enforceable in our jurisdiction?
Can we add a GDPR/data processing addendum before go-live?
What are our data portability rights at termination — can we get certified deletion?
Is a 30-day cure period negotiable for payment defaults before suspension?
actionItems: DO NOT SIGN without liability cap renegotiation
Request SLA addendum with 99.9% uptime and credit schedule
Request mutual written amendment clause for pricing and feature changes
Request GDPR / DPA addendum if any EU personal data will be processed
Have legal counsel review arbitration and class action waiver clauses
disclaimer: Synth is not legal advice or financial advice. It is a document review aid. Consult a qualified professional before making decisions.
